support_classes.supported: Supported for the listed platforms when used with the documented attribute set.
support_classes.partial: Available with backend limits, semantic caveats, or reduced attribute coverage.
support_classes.unsupported: Not part of the supported surface.

policy.tag_rule: Only tags listed in this file are claimed by the public serval-svg skill.
policy.attribute_rule: Only attributes listed under supported_attributes or partially_supported_attributes are claimed. Unlisted attributes are not part of the public contract.

global_caveats: Only the tags and attributes listed here are part of the public support contract.
Unlisted attributes should be treated as unsupported until the expert workflow audits and publishes them.
Platform differences are part of the support answer. Do not collapse them into a single optimistic claim.

partially_supported_attributes.width: Root width is commonly present in input SVG, but the renderer uses the external viewport context instead of parsing svg@width.
partially_supported_attributes.height: Root height is commonly present in input SVG, but the renderer uses the external viewport context instead of parsing svg@height.
partially_supported_attributes.opacity: Container opacity is inherited into children instead of using isolated group compositing.

caveats: The root svg element applies viewBox and then renders its children.
CSS transform inside style has special handling on the root svg element.

partially_supported_attributes.opacity: Group opacity is inherited into children instead of using isolated group compositing.

caveats: Group attributes such as fill, stroke, mask, and color are propagated to child nodes during render.

unsupported_attributes.transform: Defs is a non-rendering container; its own transform is parsed but not applied as a render step.

caveats: Defs is used as a non-rendering container for reusable nodes.

unsupported_attributes.fill: Line rendering only issues a draw call when stroke is present.
unsupported_attributes.fill-rule: Line rendering is stroke-driven; fill-rule does not affect the output.

caveats: A line is effectively stroke-only in the current renderer.

unsupported_attributes.width: Width is currently not applied when rendering a referenced node.
unsupported_attributes.height: Height is currently not applied when rendering a referenced node.
unsupported_attributes.opacity: Opacity is parsed on use but is not forwarded to the referenced node.

partially_supported_attributes.x: Image geometry is reliable for common cases, but percentage and non-px units are not fully covered.
partially_supported_attributes.y: Image geometry is reliable for common cases, but percentage and non-px units are not fully covered.
partially_supported_attributes.width: Image geometry is reliable for common cases, but percentage and non-px units are not fully covered.
partially_supported_attributes.height: Image geometry is reliable for common cases, but percentage and non-px units are not fully covered.

unsupported_attributes.fill: Image rendering does not use shape fill or stroke painting.
unsupported_attributes.stroke: Image rendering does not use shape fill or stroke painting.
unsupported_attributes.opacity: Image rendering does not fully participate in the generic opacity pipeline.
unsupported_attributes.fill-opacity: Image rendering does not fully participate in the generic opacity pipeline.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-opacity: Image rendering does not fully participate in the generic opacity pipeline.
unsupported_attributes.clip-path: clip-path behavior on image is unreliable in the current renderer.

caveats: Android, iOS, Harmony, and Skity can render images when the host app provides image loading support.

partially_supported_attributes.style: Inline style only affects the limited text properties currently supported.

unsupported_attributes.stroke: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-width: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-linecap: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-linejoin: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-dasharray: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.stroke-dashoffset: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.opacity: Text opacity is limited in the current renderer.
unsupported_attributes.fill-opacity: Text opacity is limited in the current renderer.
unsupported_attributes.clip-path: clip-path behavior on text is unreliable in the current renderer.

caveats: Android and iOS provide the current text support path.
Skity and Harmony do not currently provide usable text rendering.

partially_supported_attributes.style: Inline style only affects the limited text properties currently supported.

unsupported_attributes.transform: TSpan inherits generic node parsing but the paragraph builder does not apply per-span transforms.
unsupported_attributes.stroke: Current text support mainly covers fill color and font size.
unsupported_attributes.opacity: Current text rendering does not apply the generic opacity pipeline per span.

caveats: tspan support is limited to incremental text styling.
Linear gradients are supported through the renderer's gradient pipeline.
Android ignores a distinct radial-gradient focus point in the final shader and uses cx/cy for the center.
Pattern definitions are resolved lazily when shapes reference url(#pattern-id).
All current backends support vector pattern fill and stroke through resolver-driven tile rendering.
Pattern stroke under vector-effect="non-scaling-stroke" remains outside the current vector-effect support path.

unsupported_attributes.transform: clipPath container transforms are not fully applied.

caveats: clipPath is supported through the renderer's clip pipeline.
Masking is supported across current backends.

unsupported_attributes.x: Filter region coordinates are parsed but current backends do not use them to drive filter evaluation.
unsupported_attributes.y: Filter region coordinates are parsed but current backends do not use them to drive filter evaluation.
unsupported_attributes.width: Filter region coordinates are parsed but current backends do not use them to drive filter evaluation.
unsupported_attributes.height: Filter region coordinates are parsed but current backends do not use them to drive filter evaluation.
unsupported_attributes.filterUnits: Filter units are parsed but current backends do not consume them.
unsupported_attributes.primitiveUnits: Primitive units are parsed but current backends do not consume them.

caveats: Filters only run on backends with runtime filter support.
At present that is the Skity backend.

unsupported_attributes.in: Filter input graph selection is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.result: Filter result chaining is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.x: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.y: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.width: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.height: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.

caveats: Skity provides limited blur and drop-shadow style filter behavior.

unsupported_attributes.in: Filter input graph selection is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.result: Filter result chaining is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.x: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.y: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.width: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.height: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.

caveats: Skity uses feOffset only in limited shadow-style filter behavior.

unsupported_attributes.in: Filter input graph selection is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.result: Filter result chaining is parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.x: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.y: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.width: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.
unsupported_attributes.height: Primitive subregion coordinates are parsed but not evaluated.

caveats: Skity uses color-matrix values only in limited shadow-style filter behavior.
